_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot, FL state-leg batch)
_meta.note: FL state tier: Brodeur (SD-10), Massullo (SD-11), Garrison (HD-11 Speaker-designate), Burton (SD-12 Health Policy chair), Duggan (HD-12). Evidence from official FL Senate/House roll-call PDFs, Freedom Index, Digital Democracy, campaign sites, iVoterGuide. TWO cross-party gun deviations flagged: Massullo + Burton both voted YEA on 2018 SB7026 (red-flag/age-21/waiting-period) -> self_defense q1 FALSE. Massullo marriage-definition 'Neutral' (libertarian non-affirmation) -> q0 null. Duggan CURRENCY FIX: term-limited, NOT running 2026 (open seat). Unconfirmed individual roll calls left null rather than inferred from party.

profile.background: FL Senate District 10 (Seminole + N. Orange) since 2020; President Pro Tempore since Nov 2024. FL House 2010-2018. Healthcare-consulting/business exec; former Seminole County Chamber CEO and Seminole GOP chair.

jason-brodeur@FL.notes_set: FL State Senator, SD-10 (R), Senate President Pro Tempore. [2026-06 national-autopilot refinement, state tier] Conservative marquee record: YEA on SB300 (2023 6-week heartbeat ban, party-line 26-13); YEA on HB1 (2023 universal vouchers, party-line 26-12); YEA on HB543 (2023 permitless carry, 27-13); and as a House member voted NO on SB7026 (2018 post-Parkland red-flag/age-21/waiting-period package) -> opposed red-flag. Campaign: sponsored legislation to bar government from closing places of worship during a pandemic. No cross-party deviation found. Several 2023 marquee bills (SB254, SB1718, anti-CBDC/anti-ESG) passed with him on the GOP side but his individual roll call was not separately pulled -> left null pending cited votes.

0.note: YEA on SB300, the 2023 six-week 'Heartbeat Protection Act' (party-line 26-13).
YEA on HB1 (2023 universal school vouchers/ESAs), party-line 26-12 (only Democrats voted NO).
Campaign: sponsored legislation to stop government 'shutting down places of worship - even during a pandemic' (free-exercise/worship protection).
YEA on HB543 (2023 permitless/constitutional carry), passed 27-13.

1.note: As a House member voted NO on SB7026 (2018 Marjory Stoneman Douglas Act creating FL red-flag orders + age-21 + 3-day wait) -> opposed red-flag/restrictions.

profile.religion: Evangelical Christian (self-described 'reformed fundamental evangelical Christian'; affirms biblical inerrancy)
profile.background: FL Senate District 11 (Citrus area) since Dec 2025 special election; FL House 2016-2024 (Dist 34/23). Dermatologist/physician (WVU School of Medicine).

ralph-e-massullo-jr@FL.notes_set: FL State Senator, SD-11 (R), physician; FL House 2016-2024. [2026-06 national-autopilot refinement, state tier] Strongly pro-life: co-sponsored HB5 (2022 15-week) and the SB300/HB7 6-week ban, with a floor quote to 'respect the rights of the unborn.' Pro-biological-sex (YEA HB1421 gender-care + HB1639 'Biological Sex' definitions); pro-school-choice (YEA HB1). PRIME SPONSOR of SJR1104 (2026) protecting religious expression in public schools; sponsored DeSantis's anti-vaccine-mandate (medical-conscience) bill resisting the federal COVID mandate. TWO documented nuances: (1) on defining marriage he answered 'Neutral' - 'I don't believe Marriage should be governmentally defined' (libertarian non-affirmation, NOT pro-SSM) -> biblical_marriage q0 left null; (2) CROSS-PARTY GUN DEVIATION - as a House member voted YEA on SB7026 (2018 red-flag/age-21/waiting-period package) -> self_defense q1 FALSE; he was recorded NA (did not vote) on 2023 permitless carry HB543.

0.note: Co-sponsor/co-introducer of HB5 (2022 15-week) and the SB300/HB7 6-week ban; YEA on both; floor: 'we need to respect the rights of the unborn'; campaign: 'life begins at conception.'

Declined to affirm man-woman marriage - answered 'Neutral,' stating marriage should not be 'governmentally defined' (libertarian non-affirmation, not a pro-SSM stance). No documented contrary vote -> null.

2.note: YEA on HB1421 (2023 minor gender-care restrictions) and HB1639 (2024 'Biological Sex' definitions); opposes taxpayer-funded gender transition.

0.note: YEA on HB1 (2023 universal vouchers/ESAs) and HB3C (Family Empowerment Scholarship).
PRIME SPONSOR of SJR1104 (2026), a constitutional amendment protecting student/personnel religious expression in public schools.

1.note: Sponsored DeSantis's anti-vaccine-mandate bill protecting medical/employment conscience (right to work).
Backed the state bill blocking the federal COVID vaccine mandate (declined an unconstitutional federal mandate).

CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: voted YEA on SB7026 (2018 Marjory Stoneman Douglas Act) creating FL red-flag/risk-protection orders + age-21 purchase + 3-day waiting period - a recorded vote FOR red-flag/gun restrictions.

profile.background: FL House District 11 (Clay County) since 2020; designated House SPEAKER for 2026-2028. Attorney; former Assistant State Attorney, 4th Judicial Circuit (2001-2011); chairs Rules & Ethics.

sam-garrison@FL.notes_set: FL State Representative, HD-11 (R), Speaker-designate for 2026-2028. [2026-06 national-autopilot refinement, state tier] Consistently conservative across marquee votes; strongest dimension is public justice (former prosecutor). YEA SB300 (6-week ban) + co-sponsored 2021 abortion bill; YEA SB254 (gender-care ban) + filed an amendment on HB1557; YEA HB1 (universal vouchers) + prime-sponsored school-board term limits (HJR1461). Prime sponsor of multiple pro-police/tough-on-crime bills (HB1581 impeding LEO, HB1627 pretrial detention, HB1465 firearm offenses, HB1365 homeless-camping ban). YEA SB1718 (anti-sanctuary/E-Verify) and YEA HB543 (permitless carry). No cross-party deviation found. Economic/election/federal-overreach dimensions returned no cited individual vote -> null.

0.note: On the YEA side of SB300 (2023 6-week heartbeat ban, House 70-40); co-sponsored HB351 (2021 abortion restrictions).

2.note: On the YEA side of SB254 (2023 ban on minor gender-affirming care, House 83-28).

0.note: YEA on HB1 (2023 universal school vouchers); prime-sponsored HJR1461 (2021 school-board term limits).

2.note: Filed an amendment on and supported HB1557 (2022 Parental Rights in Education), restricting classroom SOGI instruction in K-3.

0.note: Former Assistant State Attorney; prime-sponsored HB1581 (2021) penalizing impeding/harassing law-enforcement officers (pro-police).

1.note: Prime-sponsored HB1627 (2023 Pretrial Release and Detention) tightening bail/detention (tough-on-crime, anti-cashless-bail direction).

2.note: On the YEA side of SB1718 (2023 anti-sanctuary / illegal-immigration crackdown, House 83-36).

3.note: SB1718 (which he supported) imposed the mandatory E-Verify employment-eligibility requirement.

0.note: On the YEA side of HB543 (2023 permitless/constitutional carry); prime-sponsored HB1465 (2023 firearm offenses) on the pro-gun side.

profile.background: FL Senate District 12 (Polk County) since 2022; chairs the Senate Health Policy Committee. FL House District 40 (Lakeland) 2014-2022. Nonprofit executive/consultant.

colleen-burton@FL.notes_set: FL State Senator, SD-12 (R), Senate Health Policy Committee chair; FL House 2014-2022. [2026-06 national-autopilot refinement, state tier] All scored cells confirmed from official FL Senate/House roll-call PDFs. Pro-life (YEA SB300 6-week ban, YEA HB5 15-week), with the abortion/gender bills moving through her Health Policy Committee. Pro-biological-sex (YEA SB254 gender-care ban, presided over the hearings); pro-parental-rights (YEA HB1557); pro-school-choice (YEA HB1); pro-border (YEA SB1718); pro-carry (YEA HB543). ONE documented cross-party gun deviation: as a House member voted YEA on SB7026 (2018 red-flag/age-21/waiting-period package) -> self_defense q1 FALSE. Christian-liberty, election, public-justice, and federal-overreach dimensions returned no cited individual vote -> null.

0.note: YEA on SB300 (2023 6-week ban, 26-13, 'Y Burton-12') and YEA on HB5 (2022 15-week ban, 78-39); bills moved through her Health Policy Committee.

2.note: YEA on SB254 (2023 ban on minor gender-affirming care, 26-13, 'Y Burton-12'); chaired the Health Policy hearings on it.

0.note: YEA on HB1 (2023 universal school vouchers, 26-12, 'Y Burton-12').

2.note: YEA on HB1557 (2022 Parental Rights in Education, 69-47, 'Y Burton-40'), restricting classroom SOGI instruction.
YEA on SB1718 (2023 anti-sanctuary/illegal-immigration crackdown, 27-10, 'Y Burton-12').

3.note: SB1718 (which she voted for) imposed the mandatory E-Verify employment-eligibility requirement.

0.note: YEA on HB543 (2023 permitless/constitutional carry, 27-13, 'Y Burton-12').

1.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: voted YEA on SB7026 (2018 Marjory Stoneman Douglas Act, 67-50, 'Y Burton-40') creating FL red-flag orders + age-21 + 3-day wait + bump-stock ban - a recorded vote FOR gun restrictions.

profile.background: FL House District 12 (Jacksonville/Duval) since 2018; Speaker pro tempore 2024-2026; chairs Ways & Means (prior Judiciary chair). Attorney (Rogers Towers); USMC Reserve 1993-1995. TERM-LIMITED in 2026.

wyman-duggan@FL.notes_set: FL State Representative, HD-12 (R), Speaker pro tempore. [2026-06 national-autopilot refinement, state tier] CURRENCY FIX: term-limited under Florida's 8-year limit - NOT seeking reelection; HD-12 is an OPEN seat in 2026 (Terrance Freeman running) -> candidacy_status set to not_running. Conservative record: YEA SB300 (6-week ban); supported HB1557 (parental rights); strongest dimension is public justice - PRIME SPONSOR of HB601 (2024) banning civilian police-review boards (pro-police) and YEA on SB450 (2023, 8-4 jury for death sentences). Co-sponsor + YEA on HB543 (2023 permitless carry); NRA-PVF endorsed (2022). No cross-party deviation found. Note: a libertarian Freedom Index page codes his HB1-voucher vote as 'NO' but this is a fiscal-scoring artifact and his individual final-passage vote was not independently confirmed -> school-choice cell left null. SB254/SB1718 individual roll calls not confirmed -> null.

0.note: YEA on SB300 (2023 6-week heartbeat ban, House 71-44).

2.note: Among the YES votes on HB1557 (2022 Parental Rights in Education, 69-47), restricting classroom SOGI instruction.

0.note: PRIME SPONSOR of HB601 (2024) banning civilian police-review boards - explicitly pro-police.

2.note: YEA on SB450 (2023) allowing an 8-4 jury for death sentences (proportional punishment / tough-on-crime).

0.note: Co-sponsor of and YEA on HB543 (2023 permitless/constitutional carry, House 76-32).

1.note: NRA-PVF endorsed (2022); pro-gun-rights bloc opposing new firearm restrictions.
